tables.name: app_users
tables.purpose: Stores one cloud profile row per Telegram user together with global weight settings.

columns.name: telegram_user_id
columns.notes: Unique Telegram user identifier used for account mapping.

columns.name: migrated_at
columns.notes: Set when legacy local data is first imported into Supabase.

columns.name: last_seen_at
columns.notes: Updated when the app successfully reaches the cloud layer.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.notes: Row update timestamp maintained by trigger.

tables.name: weight_entries
tables.purpose: Stores the user's complete history of weight measurements.

columns.name: id
columns.notes: Primary key that preserves existing local entry IDs.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.notes: Row update timestamp maintained by trigger.

tables.name: comments
tables.purpose: Stores user comments attached to milestones or weight entries.

columns.name: target_key
columns.notes: Milestone value or weight entry id.

columns.name: updated_at
columns.notes: Row update timestamp maintained by trigger.

relationships.notes: Each weight entry belongs to one Telegram-linked app user.
Each comment belongs to one Telegram-linked app user.

routines.name: replace_user_state
routines.purpose: Atomically replaces all weight entries for one user inside a single database transaction.

notes: The repo SQL migrations are the canonical database source of truth.
The local archive mirror is regenerated from repository artifacts and should stay in sync after every schema change.
Current production access is expected to go through validated Supabase Edge Functions even though RLS policies are already defined for future direct user-token access.
